issues.title: [AI ECOSYSTEM][EPIC] Integrate Health Assistant with safety-first local boundaries
issues.body:
## Outcome

Develop Health Assistant as an independently safe local capability that may be exposed through Quân Sư and Mama only after stricter safety, privacy, QA, and release gates pass.

## Scope

- Owner-only MVP.
- No diagnosis, prescribing, dose changes, or emergency replacement.
- No generic-chat fallback for health failures.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Intended use, hazards, evidence, privacy, contract, implementation, tests, QA, deployment, release, and maintenance are linked.
- [ ] The health capability may remain disabled while the rest of the ecosystem releases.

issues.title: [REQUIREMENTS] Define intended use, prohibited uses, and product claims
match_titles: Define intended use, prohibited uses, and product claims
issues.body:
## Outcome

Define the authoritative permitted behavior for CLI/API/Discord surfaces before health features expand.

## Scope

- Supported user and contexts, wellness tracking, symptom organization, appointment preparation, reminders from existing prescriptions, and escalation.
- Prohibited diagnosis, prescribing, dose modification, emergency replacement, medical imaging interpretation, and unsupported reassurance.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] README, API, prompts, Discord wording, tests, and release criteria use the same claims.
- [ ] Every future capability maps to an approved intended use.
- [ ] Scope expansion requires explicit safety review.

issues.title: [SAFETY] Maintain the clinical hazard log and safety ownership model
match_titles: Create the clinical hazard log and safety ownership model
issues.body:
## Outcome

Track hazards, mitigations, tests, evidence, residual risk, and accountable review for every material health behavior.

## Scope

- Missed escalation, over-escalation, medication advice, fabricated evidence, stale guidance, cross-user exposure, truncation, and provider failure.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] High-risk hazards have deterministic mitigations and regression tests.
- [ ] Residual risk is accepted explicitly before release.
- [ ] Each rule, source, prompt, and model change links to affected hazards.

issues.title: [SECURITY] Define privacy, consent, retention, export, deletion, and threat model
match_titles: Define privacy, consent, retention, and threat-model baseline
issues.body:
## Outcome

Define stricter data and trust boundaries before persistence or Discord exposure.

## Scope

- Health profile, symptoms, medication statements, allergies, observations, prompts, responses, sources, audit metadata, temporary files, backups, and provider transfer.
- Consent and provenance for every user-entered or model-proposed value.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Raw health content is excluded from operational logs.
- [ ] No model silently mutates health records.
- [ ] Export/delete and backup/restore cover all owned data.
- [ ] Cross-user and replay threats fail closed.
- [ ] Discord, gateway, assistant, and Ollama boundaries are documented.

issues.title: [EVIDENCE] Define curated medical knowledge governance and source registry
match_titles: Define curated medical knowledge governance and source registry
issues.body:
## Outcome

Define which reviewed sources may ground health support and how they are licensed, versioned, expired, replaced, and attributed.

## Scope

- Source registry schema, publisher, locale, population, version, publication/review/expiry dates, license, reviewer, content hash, status, and conflict policy.
- Open web search is not the default production source.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Unapproved, stale, withdrawn, or incompatible sources cannot enter production retrieval.
- [ ] Conflicting guidance has an explicit resolution or refusal policy.
- [ ] Every source update records review and affected safety evaluations.
- [ ] Distributable test fixtures respect source licenses.

issues.title: [EVIDENCE] Implement retrieval and claim-to-source validation
issues.body:
## Outcome

Retrieve only approved evidence and verify that every material displayed claim is supported by the cited passages.

## Scope

- Deterministic ingestion/retrieval boundaries, locale/population filters, document status, passage references, citation validation, confidence reduction, and refusal.
- Fabricated, mismatched, stale, and conflicting citation cases.

## Dependencies

- HA-AI-005

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Every material claim maps to supporting source passages.
- [ ] Missing, stale, conflicting, or mismatched evidence lowers confidence or refuses the claim.
- [ ] Fabricated citations are blocked by regression tests.
- [ ] Source removal or expiry invalidates affected retrieval results.
